description: GitHub Actions self-hosted CI via Actions Runner Controller (ARC). Ephemeral runner pods in the arc-runners namespace on Spot ci-spot capacity, observed through kube-state-metrics (kube_pod_*) and node taints (kube_node_spec_taint) — the lean node/pod-inventory slice 03-observability.sh keeps in every observability mode, so this board works across all four backends.

options.content:
## GitHub Actions (ARC) CI Observability

Real-time and historical view of the **Actions Runner Controller (ARC)** running self-hosted, **ephemeral** GitHub Actions runners in this cluster. Each queued job spins up a throwaway runner Pod in the **`arc-runners`** namespace on the **Spot `ci-spot`** capacity, then the pod is torn down — true scale-to-zero CI.

**Five signals this dashboard tracks**
- 🟢 **Health** — are runner pods completing, not crash-looping? → *Runner Pod Phase*, *Failed/Errored Runner Pods*, *Outcome Distribution*
- ⚡ **Speed** — how fast does a runner come up and finish? → *Runner Pod Age*, *Pod Scheduling Latency*
- 📦 **Capacity** — is ARC scaling up under load and back to zero when idle? → *Active Runner Pods*, *Spot CI nodes*, *Scale-up vs scale-to-zero*
- 📊 **Velocity** — how much CI is flowing? → *Runner Pod Throughput*, *Total Runner Pods*
- 📋 **Logs & Traces** — what happened in a specific job? → *Runner Pod Logs*, *Traces*, *Log Error Rate*, *Log Volume by Level*

> **Scope:** use **Status**, **Runner Scale Set**, and **Log Namespace** filters to narrow every panel · Runner membership reads from `kube_pod_*` / `kube_node_spec_taint` via kube-state-metrics, kept even in the lean/free Grafana Cloud profile — so this board works in **all four** observability backends.

**Step 1 · Read the KPI cards (top row)**
- **Active Runner Pods** — ARC runner pods running *right now* in `arc-runners` (instant gauge)
- **Runner Pods (range)** — total runner pods created in the window
- **Pod Success Rate** — 🟢 ≥ 99 % · 🟡 ≥ 80 % · 🔴 below 80 %
- **Spot CI nodes** — elastic NAP capacity backing the runners

**Step 2 · Read the trend charts**
*Runner Pods by Phase* shows Running/Succeeded/Failed/Pending over time. A rising `Failed` stack means runner pods are erroring (image pull, OOM, job failure). *Active Runner Pods vs Spot nodes* shows ARC scaling up under load and consolidating back toward **0**.

**Step 3 · Narrow with the Log level filter**
Set **Log level → error** to make *Runner Pod Logs* show only failure events from the runner pods.

**Step 4 · Drill into a Trace**
Click any **Trace ID** in *Runner Traces* to open the span waterfall for that job's instrumented steps.

**Runners not scaling up**
Jobs queued on GitHub but *Active Runner Pods* flat at 0 → the `gha-rs-controller` / listener can't reach the GitHub API or the scale set has no capacity. Check `gha_rs_controller_*` reconcile metrics and the listener pod logs in `arc-systems`.

**Pods stuck Pending (scheduling latency spike)**
A large *Runner Pod Scheduling Latency* p95 means runner pods wait for a Spot node — NAP is provisioning `ci-spot` capacity (cold-start) or hit Spot preemption / quota. Cross-check the **Node Auto-Provisioning** board.

**Runners not scaling to zero**
*Active Runner Pods* never returns to 0 between jobs → a runner is wedged (stuck job, finalizer). `kubectl get pods -n arc-runners`.

**Key metric reference**

| Metric | Type | What it tracks |
|---|---|---|
| `kube_pod_status_phase{namespace="arc-runners"}` | Gauge | Runner pods by phase |
| `kube_pod_created{namespace="arc-runners"}` | Gauge | Runner pod creation timestamp |
| `kube_pod_status_ready{namespace="arc-runners"}` | Gauge | Runner readiness |
| `gha_rs_controller_*` (if exported) | Various | gha-rs-controller reconcile activity |
| `kube_node_spec_taint{key="cloud.google.com/gke-spot"}` | Gauge | Spot CI nodes backing runners |

panels.description: Runner pods currently in Failed phase in arc-runners. Yellow ≥1, Red ≥5.
panels.title: Failed/Errored Runner Pods

panels.description: Total ARC runner pods created in the selected time range.
panels.title: Total Runner Pods (range)

panels.description: Runner pods created per hour over the selected range.
panels.title: CI Throughput (pods/h)

panels.description: Rolling share of arc-runners pods in Failed phase. Yellow >5%, Red >20%.
panels.title: Runner Pod Failure Rate

panels.description: ARC runner pods rise under load and consolidate to 0; the Spot CI nodes (NAP ci-spot) track them. The static platform pool stays flat.
panels.title: Active Runner Pods vs Spot CI Nodes (scale-up / scale-to-zero)

panels.description: Donut breakdown of arc-runners pod phases in the current snapshot.
panels.title: Runner Pod Outcome Distribution

panels.description: Runner pods by owning runner scale-set deployment (label app.kubernetes.io/instance or the ARC scale-set label).
panels.title: Active Runner Pods (by pod)
GitHub Actions / ARC — runner pods & controller (kube-state-metrics)

panels.description: ARC runner pods in Running phase (kube_pod_status_phase, ns arc-runners).
panels.title: Running Runner Pods

panels.description: ARC runner pods Pending (waiting on a Spot node to be provisioned).
panels.title: Pending Runner Pods

panels.description: Share of arc-runners pods Succeeded vs all terminal pods in snapshot.
panels.title: Runner Pod Success Rate

panels.description: Distinct ARC runner pods seen in the dashboard range.
panels.title: Total Runner Pods

panels.description: ARC runner pod count by phase over time (kube_pod_status_phase, ns arc-runners).
panels.title: Runner Pods by Phase

panels.description: Age of the oldest active runner pod (now - kube_pod_created) — a high value means a wedged / non-terminating runner.
panels.title: Runner Pod Age (oldest active)

panels.description: Actions-runner-controller reconcile activity, if the controller exports gha_rs_controller_* metrics (controller-runtime). No data = metrics not scraped (optional).
panels.title: gha-rs-controller Reconciles

panels.description: Time runner pods spend Pending before a Spot node is ready (kube_pod_status_phase Pending count is a proxy for cold-start backlog).
panels.title: Pending Pods & CI-Spot Node Readiness

panels.description: Logs from the ARC runner namespaces (the ephemeral runner pods).
panels.title: Runner Pod Logs

panels.description: Distributed traces emitted by instrumented job steps (OTLP to the collector). Drill into a single job's spans.
panels.title: Runner Traces

panels.description: Rate of error-level log lines. Spikes indicate runner/workflow pod failures.
panels.title: Log Error Rate

panels.description: Log volume broken down by severity level. Stacked view highlights relative noise across info/warn/error.
panels.title: Log Volume by Level

list.description: Filter log panels by severity (detected_level). All = everything incl. plain-text (unknown).
list.label: Log level
list.name: level

title: CI-CD / GitHub Actions (ARC) CI Observability
